text.title: Puppeteer Page Interaction (Selectors, Clicks, Forms) Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Script breaks every time the marketing team tweaks the nav layout; nth-child indexes shift.
antipattern.root-cause: Selectors generated from screenshots or codegen without attribute promotion.
antipattern.fix: Read page.content(), pick data-testid/id/name/role; if missing, file a frontend ticket to add data-testid attributes.

antipattern.symptom: Script passes locally on a fast machine, fails in CI; adding more sleeps is a losing battle.
antipattern.root-cause: page.waitForTimeout used to 'be safe'; never targets a real readiness condition.
antipattern.fix: Replace with waitForSelector or waitForFunction targeting the actual DOM/state condition that proves readiness.

antipattern.symptom: Click silently misses; form submits empty; no exception thrown.
antipattern.root-cause: page.click ran before the element was visible/enabled.
antipattern.fix: Add waitForSelector({ visible: true }) + check element.isDisabled() before the action.

antipattern.symptom: Scrape takes 8 seconds per page; logs show analytics, ad tags, video pre-loads all running.
antipattern.root-cause: Request interception not enabled; all third-party traffic runs.
antipattern.fix: page.setRequestInterception(true) and abort matches against known analytics/ads/tracking patterns.

antipattern.symptom: React form shows the field filled but submit handler sees empty value.
antipattern.root-cause: page.evaluate set element.value directly; React did not see a change event.
antipattern.fix: Use page.type for text inputs and page.select for selects. For checkboxes/radios, use page.click.
